message:
feat(demo_showcase): implement E2E headless runner (bd-35d2)

Add E2ERunner for headless scripted testing without a real TTY:

- E2ERunner struct using bubbletea ProgramSimulator
- Step DSL: press_key, press_keys, press_special, press_alt, type_text
- Mouse support: click at coordinates
- Resize simulation
- Assertion helpers: assert_contains, assert_not_contains, assert_page,
  assert_view_not_empty, assert_no_ansi
- Integration with ScenarioRecorder for artifact capture
- Config::default_test() for deterministic test config (seed=42, no animations)
- Added App::current_page() accessor for test assertions

Includes 5 unit tests demonstrating:
- Runner initialization
- Key press handling
- Page navigation (Dashboard -> Jobs -> Logs)
- View content assertions
- Window resize handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1tdh","title":"Jobs: paginator integration for long lists","description":"Add pagination for long Jobs lists using `bubbles::paginator`.

Why:
- Long lists are a common real-world case; pagination prevents the UI from becoming sluggish.
- It also showcases another Bubbles component in a realistic workflow.

Behavior:
- Paginator reflects total pages and current page.
- Works correctly with filtering/sorting:
  - applying a filter updates total pages
  - selection remains stable (best-effort) when the filtered set changes
- Keyboard:
  - next/prev page shortcuts
  - jump to first/last (optional)

Acceptance:
- Large job sets remain responsive.
- Pagination UX feels consistent with the rest of the app (styled, discoverable).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.084401297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:43.856579389Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","paginator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.103425435Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:02.336132860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1x3q","title":"Jobs: start/cancel/retry actions via Cmd/AsyncCmd","description":"Make jobs interactive.

Implement actions:
- Start a new job (creates a job + progress updates)
- Cancel job
- Retry failed job

Implementation notes:
- Use the shared domain action API (`demo_showcase data: action API …`) so UI code stays thin.
- bubbletea `Cmd` for sync simulation.
- bubbletea `AsyncCmd` when async feature enabled.

Acceptance:
- Actions produce toasts and update other pages (dashboard/logs).
- Action layer is unit tested independently of the UI.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:20.940826293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:04.939573406Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","jobs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:20.955289813Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:38.522320902Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:46.346531972Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:49.013962965Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xvj","title":"demo_showcase core: Notes scratchpad modal (bubbles::TextArea)","description":"Exercise `bubbles::TextArea` in a way that feels like a real product feature.

Feature idea:
- A modal "Notes" scratchpad to write an incident note / deploy note.
- Supports multi-line editing, copy, clear, save.
- Saving the note emits a structured log entry and a toast.

Why this belongs in core:
- Provides a compelling demo of text editing beyond single-line inputs.
- Gives users a practical pattern they can copy into real apps.

Acceptance:
- Accessible via a global key (defined in UX keymap).
- Uses `bubbles::TextArea` (not huh) so we prove both stacks.
- Saving integrates with the log stream + notifications.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:29.295951524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:46.342882675Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","textarea","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:46.342854462Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:43.822250299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:29.315341950Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:45.081658950Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:42.571125789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:41.284606640Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ywx","title":"glamour: Validate language aliases on registration","description":"# Task: Validate Language Aliases on Registration

## Problem Statement
The language_alias() builder method accepts any strings without validation:

```rust
// Lines 623-630
pub fn language_alias(mut self, alias: impl Into<String>, language: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    self.syntax_config
        .language_aliases
        .insert(alias.into(), language.into());
    self
}
```

No checks for:
1. Whether target language is actually supported
2. Whether alias forms a cycle (A -> B -> A)
3. Whether alias shadows a built-in language name

## Impact Analysis
- **Silent failures**: Alias to non-existent language just doesn't highlight
- **Confusing behavior**: Cycles could cause infinite loops or unexpected results
- **Debugging difficulty**: No error message explains why highlighting isn't working

## Solution Design

### Validation on Registration
```rust
pub fn language_alias(
    mut self,
    alias: impl Into<String>,
    language: impl Into<String>,
) -> Result<Self, GlamourError> {
    let alias = alias.into();
    let language = language.into();
    
    // Check target language exists
    if !self.syntax_set_contains(&language) {
        return Err(GlamourError::InvalidLanguage(language));
    }
    
    // Check for cycles
    if self.would_create_cycle(&alias, &language) {
        return Err(GlamourError::AliasCycle { alias, language });
    }
    
    self.syntax_config.language_aliases.insert(alias, language);
    Ok(self)
}

fn would_create_cycle(&self, alias: &str, target: &str) -> bool {
    let mut current = target;
    let mut visited = HashSet::new();
    visited.insert(alias);
    
    while let Some(next) = self.syntax_config.language_aliases.get(current) {
        if !visited.insert(next.as_str()) {
            return true;  // Cycle detected
        }
        current = next;
    }
    false
}
```

### Backward Compatibility Option
Keep existing method but add validated version:
```rust
// Existing (for compatibility)
pub fn language_alias(self, alias: impl Into<String>, language: impl Into<String>) -> Self

// New validated version
pub fn try_language_alias(
    self,
    alias: impl Into<String>,
    language: impl Into<String>,
) -> Result<Self, GlamourError>
```

## Implementation Steps
1. Add validation logic to language_alias()
2. Add GlamourError variants for invalid language and cycles
3. Consider deprecating unchecked version
4. Update documentation with valid language list
5. Add tests for validation

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn alias_to_invalid_language_fails() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("rs", "nonexistent");
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(GlamourError::InvalidLanguage(_))));
}

#[test]
fn alias_cycle_detected() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("a", "b")?
        .try_language_alias("b", "c")?
        .try_language_alias("c", "a");  // Creates cycle
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(GlamourError::AliasCycle { .. })));
}

#[test]
fn valid_alias_succeeds() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("rs", "rust");
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Invalid target language detected with clear error
- [ ] Alias cycles detected and rejected
- [ ] Backward-compatible API (old method still works)
- [ ] Documentation lists valid language names
- [ ] Tests cover validation scenarios","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.883578694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.902063497Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","glamour","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ywx","depends_on_id":"bd-8wlh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.902020466Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-201t","title":"demo_showcase page: Files (filepicker + preview)","description":"A file browser screen with a preview pane — like a real “open a config/log file” tool.

Why:
- File navigation + preview is a common real-world TUI use case.
- It’s a perfect integration demo:
  - `bubbles::filepicker` for navigation
  - `bubbles::viewport` for preview scrolling
  - `glamour` for markdown preview
  - `lipgloss` for two-pane layout + styling
  - mouse scroll and focus interactions

Must showcase:
- File picker:
  - directory navigation, back/forward, open/select
  - filtering by allowed types (e.g., md, toml, log)
  - show hidden toggle (if supported) or a demo-level filter
- Preview pane:
  - plain text preview with wrapping toggle
  - markdown preview via glamour for `.md`
  - stable scrolling independent of the picker
- Robust UX:
  - clear error handling (permissions, unreadable files, too-large files)
  - toast/status feedback for actions and errors
  - mouse wheel scroll works naturally for the focused pane

Data requirements:
- Use a deterministic fixtures root in demo/test modes so E2E can reliably navigate known files.
- Include a few curated fixture files (markdown, toml, log, and an “error” case) as part of the plan.

Acceptance:
- Feels like a real tool (fast, predictable, and pleasant).
- Preview is readable at multiple terminal sizes.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - selection + navigation state transitions
  - preview selection logic (markdown vs text)
  - error mapping (permission -> friendly message)
- E2E:
  - file picker navigate + preview scenario (verifies both text and markdown preview, captures artifacts).

Notes:
- Keep the UX crisp: this page should be *immediately* useful on its own.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:21.306244015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:42:42.981587564Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-201t","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:21.327764835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
